mark_scheme_working: AB=B-A=(-7,-7,7), so L1 can use direction (1,1,-1): r=(7,4,0)+lambda(1,1,-1)
equate two components and solve: mu=2, lambda=-2
D lies on L2 with mu=1

question: 7(a-c)
topic: parametric area and volume of revolution
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-5, ms/page-5, sol/page-4..5
question_text: Parametric curve x=cos(2theta), y=sec(theta), 0<theta<pi/2. Region under curve from x=0 to x=1/2. Show area integral int_{pi/6}^{pi/4} 4sin(theta)dtheta, evaluate exactly, then find volume when rotated about the x-axis.
mark_scheme_working: x=0 gives theta=pi/4 and x=1/2 gives theta=pi/6
area=int y dx = int_{pi/6}^{pi/4} sec(theta)*2sin(2theta)dtheta = int_{pi/6}^{pi/4} 4sin(theta)dtheta
volume=pi*int y^2 dx = pi*int sec(theta)^2*(-2sin(2theta))dtheta with limits reversed
notes: diagram only identifies bounds; integration is deterministic

question: 9(a-b)

question_text: Parametric curve x=6/t, y=6t-t^2, t!=0. Curve crosses x-axis at R. P(6,5) lies on C and normal l at P crosses x-axis at Q. Determine t at R and P, equation of l, coordinates R,Q, then use parametric integration to find shaded area to two decimals.

at t=1, tangent gradient=-2/3, so normal gradient=3/2
area under curve from t=6 to t=1 is int_6^1 (6t-t^2)(-6/t^2) dt = -30+36ln6

notes: binomial expansion and coefficient extraction

mark_scheme_working: integrate from y=1 to y and x=1 to x
notes: separable DE; y>0 avoids sign ambiguity

question: 7(a-d)
topic: 3D vectors line midpoint and acute angle
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-4, sol/page-4..5; c4_m_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Line l passes through (4,-1,1) and (-1,4,6). C=(4,-2,-3), D=(p,q,-1). Midpoint M of CD lies on l. Find vector equation of l, coordinates of M, p,q, and acute angle theta between CD and l.
mark_scheme_working: cos obtuse angle=-4/sqrt42, obtuse angle=128.1 degrees
notes: partial because angle orientation/context is geometric; vector arithmetic is deterministic

question: 8(a-b)
topic: implicit logarithmic differentiation and turning point
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-4, sol/page-5; c4_m_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Curve e^y=(x^2+3)/(x-1). Show dy/dx=((x-3)(x+1))/((x^2+3)(x-1)). Find exact coordinates of turning point.
mark_scheme_working: turning point has dy/dx=0, so x=-1 or x=3
x=-1 gives e^y=-2, impossible since e^y>0
notes: deterministic implicit differentiation and stationary point

review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-2, sol/page-1; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Use substitution u^2=1-x^2, or otherwise, show int_0^1 5x(1-x^2)^(3/2) dx=1.
mark_scheme_working: substitution route: u^2=1-x^2, so u=(1-x^2)^(1/2) and -u/x du=dx
limits x=0 -> u=1 and x=1 -> u=0
integral becomes int_1^0 -5u^4 du = int_0^1 5u^4 du
notes: deterministic substitution or reverse-chain integral

question: 2(a-b)
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-2, sol/page-1..2; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: For f(x)=2sqrt(1+4x)+4/(1+x), combine first 4 terms of (1+x)^-1 and (1+4x)^(1/2) to show f(x)≈6+4x^3. State validity range.
mark_scheme_working: validity is tightest of |4x|<1 and |x|<1, so -1/4<x<1/4
notes: combined series cancellation

notes: deterministic parametric differentiation and elimination; solution page final line omits the square in text but preceding algebra and question require (x-y)^2

question: 5(a-b)
topic: implicit differentiation and vertical tangent exclusion
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-3, sol/page-3..4; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Curve C has implicit equation y=(2x+1)/(xy+3). Find dy/dx in terms of x,y. Show there is no point on C where tangent is parallel to y-axis.
mark_scheme_working: discriminant (-2)^2-4*3*6=-68<0, so no real points with infinite gradient
expected_equivalent: no real solution for vertical tangent condition
notes: deterministic implicit differentiation plus discriminant check

question: 6(a-d)
topic: 3D vectors line membership perpendicularity and distance constraint
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-3, sol/page-4..5; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: A=(5,-1,-1), B=(1,-5,7), C=(4,-2,1). Find vector equation of line l through A,B; show C lies on l; show OC is perpendicular to l; D lies on l and |CD|=2|CA|. Find two possible coordinates of D.
mark_scheme_working: C lies on l at lambda=-1
OC=(4,-2,1) and direction=(1,1,-2), dot product=4-2-2=0, so perpendicular
lambda=1 gives D=(6,0,-3); lambda=-3 gives D=(2,-4,5)
expected_equivalent: C is on l at lambda=-1
notes: partial because geometric interpretation is worded, but vector arithmetic and final coordinates are deterministic

topic: volume of revolution with trigonometric integration
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-4, sol/page-6; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Region under y=sec x+4cos x from x=0 to x=pi/6 is rotated about the x-axis. Show volume is (pi/3)(8pi+7sqrt(3)).
mark_scheme_working: expand: sec^2 x+8sec x cos x+16cos^2 x = sec^2 x+8+16cos^2 x
use cos^2 x=(1+cos 2x)/2, giving integrand sec^2 x+16+8cos 2x
notes: deterministic expansion and definite integral; diagram only identifies bounds already stated in text

question: 8(a-b)
topic: separable differential equation with partial fractions and extrema
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-4, sol/page-6..8; c4_n_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Population P thousands satisfies dP/dt=(1/20)P(2P-1)cos t, initial P=8. Solve to show P=8/(16-15e^((1/20)sin t)); find maximum and minimum population.
mark_scheme_working: since -1<=sin t<=1, maximum occurs at sin t=1 and minimum at sin t=-1
notes: deterministic DE and extrema over sin t range; P is measured in thousands

question: 3(a-b)
topic: implicit differentiation tangent and parallel tangent point
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-3, sol/page-2; c4_o_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: Curve x^2+4xy+2y^2=7. Show dy/dx=-(x+2y)/(2x+2y), tangent at P(1,1) is 3x+4y=7, and find Q where tangent is parallel to tangent at P.
mark_scheme_working: parallel tangent has same gradient, so -(x+2y)/(2x+2y)=-3/4
substitute y=x in curve: x^2+4x^2+2x^2=7, so x^2=1
notes: deterministic implicit differentiation and simultaneous equations

question: 5(a-d)
topic: 3D vector lines perpendicular intersection and chord midpoint
review_basis: manual page-image review: q/page-4, sol/page-4..5; c4_o_marks.pdf is Coming Soon placeholder
question_text: A=(3,0,3), B=(4,-1,5). Find line l1 through A,B. Line l2=(5,10,4)+mu(1,3,1). Show l1 and l2 perpendicular, intersect at P, and given C=(0,-5,-1) on l2 find D where circle centered at E on l1 cuts l2 at C,D.
mark_scheme_working: intersection solves lambda+3=mu+5 and -lambda=3mu+10; adding gives 3=4mu+15, so mu=-3
because center E lies on l1 and l1 is perpendicular to l2 through P, P is midpoint of chord CD
with C=(0,-5,-1), midpoint P=(2,1,1) gives D=(4,7,3)
notes: partial because circle chord midpoint reasoning is geometric; vector arithmetic is deterministic
